title: From Raw Inputs to Production-Ready AI Data
objective: See how ingestion, dataset shape, and ownership determine product reliability later on.
beginnerExplanation: Every AI system depends on a data path. Documents, events, annotations, or feedback have to be collected, cleaned, enriched, versioned, and moved into the systems that use them.
deeperExplanation: The strongest teams treat data plumbing as a product-quality function. They track freshness, ownership, schema drift, and rollout risk because small upstream issues can create large downstream failures in retrieval, evals, or user-facing outputs.
keyTakeaways: Data contracts make AI systems more stable.
Versioning matters for datasets and prompts, not just models.
Upstream quality issues usually show up later as confusing product failures.
example: A support automation product ingests tickets, sanitizes PII, enriches metadata, versions training and eval snapshots, and alerts the team when schema changes break extraction logic.
commonMistakes: Treating ingestion as a one-time setup task.
Skipping versioning for datasets and annotations.
Losing ownership when multiple teams touch the same data path.
exercise: Document the upstream inputs, transformations, ownership rules, and freshness checks for a retrieval or eval pipeline you would trust in production.
